info.title: RiskModels API
info.description:
Precision equity risk intelligence platform with AI agent integration.
Institutional-grade equity risk analysis for AI agents and quantitative applications. Provides daily factor hedge ratios, explained-risk decompositions, and portfolio-level risk attribution for ~3,000 US equities.

**New in v3.0.0-agent:**
- OAuth2 client credentials flow for machine-to-machine auth
- Plaid Investments integration for live portfolio sync
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for AI agent tools
- Compliance manifests for AI marketplace integration
- Per-key rate limiting and scoped access control

**Data**: Universe `uni_mc_3000` (top ~3,000 US stocks by market cap), date range 2006-01-04 to present, updated daily end-of-day. Backend: Zarr v2 on Google Cloud Storage, computed by the ERM3 regression system (Huber/Ridge). Supabase tables `erm3_betas` and `erm3_rankings` provide factor betas and ticker rankings for direct DB and API use.

**Authentication Methods:**
1. Session cookies (web app)
2. API Key/Secret via Bearer token (external integrations)
3. OAuth2 client credentials (AI agents, npm package)

**Security Features:**
- GCP KMS envelope encryption for portfolio data
- Cryptographic shredding on key deletion
- HMAC-SHA256 webhook verification
- Row-level security (RLS) via Supabase

**Pricing**: Per-request billing with prepaid balance. Cached responses are free. Many metered responses include `_agent.cost_usd` in the JSON body; others bill silently — always check the `X-API-Cost-USD` response header when present.
**Lineage**: All data responses include `_metadata` (model version, data_as_of, factor set) and headers `X-Risk-Model-Version`, `X-Data-As-Of`, `X-Factor-Set-Id`, `X-Universe-Size`.

BearerAuth.description: API key in format `rm_agent_{live|test}_{random}_{checksum}` or `rm_user_{random}_{checksum}`. Obtain via `POST /api/auth/provision` or from riskmodels.app/settings → API Keys. Include as: `Authorization: Bearer rm_agent_live_...`

scopes.ticker-returns: Access ticker returns and historical data

AgentMeta.description: Per-request billing and telemetry metadata appended to metered responses.

cost_usd.description: Cost deducted from prepaid balance for this request. 0 if served from cache.

request_id.description: Unique request identifier for support lookups.

data_freshness.description: ISO timestamp of the data's as-of datetime.

billing_code.description: Internal billing classification for this request type.

cache_status.description: Whether this response was served from cache.

RiskMetadata.description: Lineage and provenance metadata for all data responses. Included in response body as _metadata and as X-Risk-* headers.

universe_size.description: Number of stocks in the universe.

EstimateResponse.description: Cost estimate for a request before it is made.

estimated_rows.description: Estimated row count for time-series endpoints.

note.example: Actual cost may vary. Cached responses are free.

MetricsV3.description: V3 metric keys as returned inside `GET /metrics/{ticker}` under the `metrics` property. Suffixes: `_mkt` market, `_sec` sector, `_sub` subsector, `_res` residual. Hedge ratios (HR) are dollars of ETF to trade per $1 of stock; explained risk (ER) are variance fractions (0–1).

vol_23d.description: Annualised realised volatility (23 trading days), decimal fraction.

MetricsSnapshotResponse.description: Wire response for `GET /metrics/{ticker}`. Numeric fields live under `metrics`; `teo` is the observation date (V3 time-end).

symbol.description: Canonical symbol key in V3 registry.

teo.description: Latest observation date for the metrics row.

display.description: Optional display labels for metric keys (e.g. UI column titles).

TickerReturnsDailyRow.description: One trading day in `GET /ticker-returns` `data` array (V3 security_history fields).

returns_gross.description: Daily gross return of the stock.

l3_mkt_hr.description: Rolling L3 market hedge ratio (SPY-notional per $1 stock).

BatchHedgeRatios.description: Present when `metrics` includes `hedge_ratios`. Dollar HRs per $1 stock (same economics as `full_metrics` `*_hr` fields). Property names are **short** (`l1_market`, …); ERM3 zarr hedge-weight naming `L1_market_HR` … `L3_subsector_HR` maps row-wise to these keys — see docs/ERM3_ZARR_API_PARITY.md.

BatchReturnsPayload.description: Present when `metrics` includes `returns`. Arrays are parallel by index. Fields `l1`, `l2`, `l3` hold daily rolling hedge ratios from V3 (`l3_mkt_hr`, `l3_sec_hr`, `l3_sub_hr` history), not combined L1/L2/L3 model levels.

BatchFullMetrics.description: Present when `metrics` includes `full_metrics`. Flat snapshot for batch consumers: L1/L2/L3 explained-risk and hedge-ratio fields when available from the backend (see docs/ERM3_ZARR_API_PARITY.md for zarr `L*_ER` / `L*_HR` mapping). Zarr topic-level `wmean_*` features are computed **client-side** as holdings-weighted means of these fields. Nulls mean missing or not modelled for that ticker — not omitted keys.

date.description: Observation date (`teo`) for the snapshot; align with `X-Data-As-Of` / `_metadata.data_as_of`.

BatchAnalyzeTickerResult.description: One entry in `POST /batch/analyze` `results` map (keyed by upper-case ticker).

_metadata.description: Lineage for PIT tests: model version, as-of date, factor set, universe size. Also emitted as `X-Risk-Model-Version`, `X-Data-As-Of`, `X-Factor-Set-Id`, `X-Universe-Size` headers when the gateway attaches them.

L3DecompositionResponse.description: Columnar L3 decomposition from V3 `security_history` (daily points; arrays are parallel by index). Field names use readable suffixes (`l3_market_er`, …). No `_agent` block in the current response body.

message.description: Human-readable error description with suggested action.
message.example: Ticker 'XYZABC' not found in universe 'uni_mc_3000'. Check ticker spelling or try a different universe.

suggestion.example: Check for typos or use ticker search endpoint

access_token.description: JWT access token (use as Bearer token)

RiskMetrics.description: Optional risk enrichment on Plaid holdings; names may match batch `full_metrics` or V3 `metrics` keys depending on pipeline version.

MCPTool.description: MCP tool descriptor as returned by `tools/list`. Tools are discoverable via `https://riskmodels.app/.well-known/mcp.json` or by connecting to the SSE endpoint at `https://riskmodels.app/api/mcp/sse` and calling `tools/list`.

name.description:
Available tools:
- `riskmodels_list_endpoints`: List all available API endpoints with summaries, tags, and costs. No required inputs.
- `riskmodels_get_capability`: Get detailed schema and documentation for a specific capability by ID (e.g. "ticker-returns", "metrics", "l3-decomposition"). Input: `{ id: string }`.
- `riskmodels_get_schema`: Fetch the JSON response schema for a given endpoint path. Input: `{ path: string }`.
- `analyze_portfolio`: Analyze a portfolio of ticker positions — returns per-ticker risk metrics (volatility, Sharpe, hedge ratios, explained risk) and portfolio-level aggregates. Input: `{ positions: Array<{ ticker: string, weight?: number }> }`.
- `hedge_portfolio`: Compute optimal hedge notionals for a portfolio using ERM3 factor model. Returns L1/L2/L3 hedge amounts in USD. Input: `{ positions: Array<{ ticker: string, notional_usd: number }>, level?: "l1"|"l2"|"l3" }`.
- `get_risk_decomposition`: Get monthly L3 factor risk decomposition time series for a ticker. Input: `{ ticker: string, from?: string, to?: string }`.

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters.

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema describing the tool's output structure.

RankingMetricKeys.description: V3 security_history ranking metric keys (when rankings are exposed via API). Pattern: rank_ord_{window}_{cohort}_{metric}, cohort_size_{window}_{cohort}_{metric}. Windows: 1d, 21d, 63d, 252d. Cohorts: universe, sector, subsector. Metrics: mkt_cap, gross_return, sector_residual, subsector_residual, er_l1, er_l2, er_l3. rank_percentile computed client-side: (1 - (rank_ord - 1) / cohort_size) * 100.

InsufficientBalance.description: Returned when the account prepaid balance is exhausted (HTTP 402).

message.example: Insufficient balance. Current balance: $0.00. Top up at https://riskmodels.app/billing

RateLimitExceeded.description: Returned when the per-minute rate limit is exceeded (HTTP 429).

retry_after.description: ISO timestamp after which the client may retry.

limit.description: Requests allowed per minute for this key.

post.description:
OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow for machine-to-machine authentication. Exchange API credentials for a short-lived JWT token (15 minutes).

**Use Cases:**
- AI agents connecting to the API
- Server-to-server integrations
- npm package authentication
- MCP client authentication

Send JSON-RPC 2.0 messages to the MCP server.

**Available methods:**
- `tools/list` - List available MCP tools
- `tools/call` - Invoke an MCP tool
- `resources/list` - List available resources
- `resources/read` - Read a resource

get.description: Plugin manifest for OpenAI GPT Store integration. Enables ChatGPT and other OpenAI models to discover and use the RiskModels API.

Comprehensive data handling and privacy disclosure for AI marketplace compliance. Documents encryption, retention, third-party sharing, and regulatory compliance.

**Key sections:**
- Data handling (AES-256-GCM, GCP KMS)
- Cryptographic shredding for GDPR compliance
- Third-party sharing policy (none)
- AI model usage (no training on user data)
- Compliance (GDPR, SOC2, PCI-DSS)
Model Context Protocol server connection manifest. Enables AI model clients to discover and connect to the RiskModels MCP server.

Returns enriched holdings from connected Plaid accounts with risk metrics.

**Features:**
- Automatic ticker resolution and normalization
- Risk enrichment with factor exposures
- Real-time portfolio valuation
- Multi-account aggregation

**Authentication:** Required (API Key or OAuth2 with plaid:holdings scope)
**Billing:** $0.01 per request
**Rate Limit:** 60 requests/minute

Returns the latest V3 daily metrics for the ticker. Numeric fields are under the `metrics` object using abbreviated keys (`l3_mkt_hr`, `l3_sec_hr`, `l3_sub_hr`, `l3_mkt_er`, `l3_res_er`, `vol_23d`, etc.). The observation date is `teo`. Optional `display` maps keys to human-readable labels.

**Authentication:** Required (API Key or OAuth2)
**Billing:** $0.005 per request (deducted from account balance)
**Rate Limit:** 60 requests/minute (default)

404.description: Symbol not found or no metrics row (see error message).

error.example: Rate limit exceeded. Try again at 2026-03-07T12:34:56Z

get.summary: Daily returns time series with rolling hedge ratios
get.description: Returns a daily time series of gross stock returns (`returns_gross`) and V3 rolling hedge ratios and explained-risk fields per day: `l3_mkt_hr`, `l3_sec_hr`, `l3_sub_hr`, `l3_mkt_er`, `l3_sec_er`, `l3_sub_er`, `l3_res_er`. Up to 15 years per request. For the full L1/L2/L3 **snapshot** (all six HR names on the latest day), use `GET /metrics/{ticker}` (`metrics` object). Response does not include a top-level `_agent` block; billing is applied server-side. Cost: $0.005/call regardless of years pulled.

parameters.name: years
parameters.description: Number of years of history to return.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

parameters.name: nocache
parameters.description: Bypass cache (incurs cost even if identical request was recently made).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Response format. `json` (default) returns `TickerReturnsResponseV3` (symbol, ticker, `data` rows with V3 field names, `meta`). Export formats may differ — confirm column names in your client when using `parquet` or `csv`.

200.description: Time series of daily returns and V3 rolling HR/ER fields.

get.summary: Daily gross returns time series (single ticker)
get.description: Returns daily gross returns for a single stock. Simpler than `/ticker-returns` (no hedge ratios). Cost: $0.005/call.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Response format. Parquet/CSV return tabular (date, returns_gross).

get.description: Returns daily gross returns for an ETF (e.g. SPY, XLK). Cost: $0.005/call.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Response format. Parquet/CSV return tabular (date, returns_gross).

get.description: Returns columnar time series of L3 hedge ratios and explained-risk fractions (parallel arrays by trading date) from V3 `security_history`. Components: market, sector, subsector, residual. Includes `ticker`, `universe`, and `data_source`. This route does not append `_agent` to the JSON body in the current implementation; use headers / account balance for billing if applicable.

parameters.name: market_factor_etf
parameters.description: Market factor ETF (passed through to the decomposition service).

post.description: Fetch data for up to 100 tickers in one JSON response (`results` map keyed by ticker). Supported `metrics` flags: `returns` (daily arrays with `l1`/`l2`/`l3` columns — see operation schema for semantics), `hedge_ratios` (latest six hedge ratios with **short** keys `l1_market`, …), `full_metrics` (flat L1/L2/L3 ER/HR snapshot with **long** keys `l1_market_hr`, `l1_market_er`, …). Request every block you need: `metrics` is a whitelist (no silent backfill). For ERM3 zarr parity (`L*_ER` / `L*_HR`), request `["full_metrics","hedge_ratios"]` and see docs/ERM3_ZARR_API_PARITY.md. The `l3_decomposition` flag is accepted but may be unpopulated depending on deployment. Cost: $0.002/position, minimum $0.01/call.

metrics.description: Which payloads to include per ticker (`full_metrics` recommended for portfolio screens).

years.description: Number of years of history (applies to `returns` and `l3_decomposition`).

format.description: Parquet/CSV export shapes may differ from JSON; validate columns in your pipeline.

200.description: Batch results keyed by ticker (JSON) or export formats when implemented.

X-Risk-Model-Version.description: ERM3 model version (align with zarr slice / `_metadata.model_version`).

X-Data-As-Of.description: Latest trading date for the batch snapshot.

X-Universe-Size.description: Universe count for this model build.

400.description: Invalid request body or too many tickers (> 100).

get.description: List tickers in the universe or search by name/symbol. Free endpoint (no charge). Use the `mag7` flag to retrieve the MAG7 tickers. Use `include_metadata` for sector and ETF assignment per ticker.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: `ticker` returns all ticker symbols. `teo` returns valid trading dates.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search string matched against ticker symbol or company name.

parameters.name: mag7
parameters.description: Return only MAG7 tickers (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA).

parameters.name: include_metadata
parameters.description: Include sector, sector ETF, and subsector ETF per ticker.

post.description: Returns predicted cost before a request is made. Free to call, requires authentication. Used by AI agents for pre-flight cost checks.

params.description: Same params as the target endpoint

get.description: Returns current service status, version, and capability availability. Free, no auth required.
Returns current prepaid balance, account status, and rate-limit settings for the authenticated token.
Returns paginated invoice history and a summary of spend by period (month/quarter/year).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of invoices to return.

post.description: Exchange a valid session JWT for a long-lived Bearer API key in format `rm_agent_{live|test}_{random}_{checksum}`. Intended for AI agents that need a stable key for repeated calls.

get.description: Performance and reliability metrics for API capabilities. Optional filter by capability and number of days. Cost: $0.002/call.

parameters.name: capability
parameters.description: Specific capability id to get metrics for.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Number of days of historical data.

200.description: Telemetry metrics for the requested period.

post.description: Natural language risk analysis via conversational AI (GPT-4). Request body includes messages array and optional model (default gpt-4o-mini). Billed per token.

200.description: Assistant reply and optional tool calls.
